mechanism_narrative: Low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of periods when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and weakening consumer spending power, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >60% of instances following a 0.5pp unemployment rate increase, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which translates into positive corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive deviation of RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side momentum, generating positive short-term S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to precede positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of observations during early-cycle phases, or the predictive IC drops below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price inflation above 3.5% forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, increasing discount rates and compressing equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in >60% of periods when CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5%, or the inflation threshold fails to separate return distributions significantly.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a stable or declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of PAYEMS growth and UNRATE stability does not yield positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product reflects stronger aggregate economic activity and corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates fail to predict positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in >55% of quarterly observations, or the Rank IC remains below 0.02 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly industrial production decline exceeding 1.0% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output and supply chain stress, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances following a 1.0% monthly INDPRO drop, or the threshold condition shows no predictive power in walk-forward tests.
